name: alpha_satellite_suprachromosomal_families_I_to_V
description: Architectural archetype: human alpha-satellite suprachromosomal families. Alpha-satellite monomers fall into five suprachromosomal families (SFs 1-5) based on monomer sequence type (J1, J2 for SF1; W1-W5 for SF2; M1, M2 for SF3; D1, D2 for SF4; R1, R2 for SF5). Active centromeric HORs typically belong to SF1-3, while flanking monomeric/diverged alpha-sat is enriched for SF4-5.

architecture.internal_structure: Five monomer-type families (SF1-SF5) defined by sequence (Alexandrov 1988); each chromosome's active centromere uses a specific HOR built from 1-2 SFs; pericentric arms contain older, more diverged SF4-5 monomers; layered organisation reflects sequential centromere evolution.
